email_summary: Sierra Verde Coffee Cooperative supplied 10 tons of green Arabica coffee in Q1 (arrived Ashdod ~Feb 27, scored 85.2) and 10 tons in Q2 (arrived June 6, scored 85.8 — best ever received). Both shipments experienced minor delays (1–4 days) but remained within Strauss's planning windows. Q3 is under discussion for August–September delivery of another 10 tons from the La Esperanza plot, with no pricing or documentation yet initiated.

latest_price_quoted.display: No price quoted in emails — contract amendment price $4,450/ton applies

contract_terms.key_penalty: Late delivery beyond 10 calendar days past agreed delivery window incurs 0.5% of shipment value per week delayed.

open_issues.description: The contract expires 2026-08-31, and Q3 is being planned for August–September delivery — renewal negotiation has not been initiated and the contract could lapse mid-Q3 shipment cycle without prompt action.
open_issues.source_ref: Contract STR-COFFEE-2024-003, Effective Date and Term section; email 2026-06-17 from Alejandro Reyes (Q3 discussion)

open_issues.description: Q3 volume (10 tons, August–September) was proposed by Alejandro on 2026-06-17 but David has not yet replied to confirm, and Sofia has not been asked to start documentation or scheduling.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-06-17 from Alejandro Reyes, subject: RE: Q3 — and some news from the farm

open_issues.description: Alejandro flagged twice (2026-01-05 and 2026-06-17) that USD invoicing could create an issue for Strauss if the ILS/USD rate moves, and offered to 'figure something out together' — no formal currency or payment accommodation has been documented or agreed in writing.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-01-05 from Alejandro Reyes; email 2026-06-17 from Alejandro Reyes

open_issues.description: The contract requires firm calendar delivery dates, but Q3 delivery window has been communicated only as 'August–September delivery' with no confirmed ship date or arrival date — the contract notes that vague windows must be flagged and confirmed ahead of each shipment.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-06-17 from Alejandro Reyes; contract STR-COFFEE-2024-003 Notes section

commodity_benchmark: No benchmark in scope for this category
heads_up: The contract expires 2026-08-31 — the same month Q3 shipment is expected to depart Colombia — so Strauss must initiate renewal discussions now or risk a lapse in contractual coverage during an active shipment. Additionally, no firm Q3 ship date or arrival date has been confirmed, which is required by the contract's delivery terms and creates penalty-clause ambiguity if delays occur.
negotiation_brief: This is a high-performing, low-friction supplier relationship: two consecutive shipments have delivered above the 84-point contract minimum (85.2 and 85.8), volumes have matched the 10-ton commitment each quarter, and the cooperative has been responsive and transparent about minor delays. The contract is active and in good standing, with the amended price of $4,450/ton holding — no new pricing has been proposed in emails, so there is no active price discrepancy. The single most important issue walking in is the August 31 renewal deadline: Q3 is being planned for August–September, meaning the contract could expire during or just before the shipment, and without a signed renewal Strauss loses penalty-clause protection and volume commitment guarantees. Secure a 24-month renewal at current terms before confirming Q3, and lock in a firm Q3 ship date in writing to satisfy the contract's delivery-date requirement.

financial_signals.delivery_reliability_score: Mixed — minor delays on both Q1 (1 day, phytosanitary inspector) and Q2 (4 days, dry mill equipment), but both shipments arrived within Strauss's planning windows and well within the 10-day penalty threshold.

raw_emails.subject: Feliz Año Nuevo, David! + Harvest update
raw_emails.body:
David!

Happy new year from Huila! We had a wonderful holidays here, the family is well. How are Sara and the kids? I keep thinking about when you came to visit us in 2023 — you have to come back for the next harvest, the community would love to see you again. Diego from the field team still talks about the cupping you did with us.

On the business side — the January-February harvest is looking very good this year, maybe the best since 2021. We are expecting strong yield from the La Esperanza plot (the one you tasted at the cupping). I believe we can fully honor the Q1 commitment with excellent quality, probably 84.5-85 cup score.

One thing I should mention — all Sierra Verde invoices are in USD, as you know. I just want to flag in case the ILS/USD rate ever creates a problem for your team. If timing of payment ever causes an issue, just tell me and we can talk about it. We are flexible with people we trust.

Let me know when you want to plan the Q1 delivery.

Alejandro

raw_emails.subject: RE: Feliz Año Nuevo + Q1 planning
raw_emails.body:
Alejandro!

Happy new year to you and all the family. Yes, that visit was a highlight — I'll make it happen again, maybe late 2026.

Great news on the harvest. Q1 we're planning 10 tons. Can you do early February departure from Bogotá? Ideally arrive here around the 20th.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 planning — details
raw_emails.body:
David,

Perfect. 10 tons, we can do. Sofia here manages our logistics and export side — she will handle the documentation and coordinate with the freight forwarder. I'm copying her now.

On timing: harvest processing will be complete by January 28. So realistically the earliest we could dispatch from Bogotá is February 3-4. By sea that puts you at approximately February 22-25. Is that workable?

Also — my wife sends her greetings. She is teaching again this year, back at the school in Pitalito. The kids here are always asking her about the world outside Colombia. She tells them about David from Israel who visited the farm and thought our coffee was worth traveling across the world for! I don't think she is exaggerating too much.

Alejandro

raw_emails.subject: Export documentation — Q1 shipment
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

This is Sofia from Sierra Verde, handling logistics. To start the export process, could you confirm the destination port and your customs broker contact? I'll get the phytosanitary certificate and ICO certificate of origin started. Target ship date is February 3 from Bogotá.

Thanks,
Sofia

raw_emails.subject: RE: Export documentation
raw_emails.body:
Sofia,

Destination: Ashdod port, Israel. Customs broker: Katz Shipping Ltd, contact: miri.katz@katzshipping.co.il.

Thanks,
David

raw_emails.subject: From the farm + ship date
raw_emails.body:
David,

I am writing from the road today — I'm actually visiting some of our smaller farms in Pitalito with Diego this week, watching the harvest come in. The green here is incredible right now. I wish you could see it.

On the Q1 shipment — we had a small delay at the export warehouse. The phytosanitary inspector came two days late, it is always something with the government schedule. So the actual ship date will be February 6, one day later than planned. ETA at Ashdod shifts to around February 26-27. Sofia is sending the B/L today.

This is still within your window, yes? I hope so. The quality on this lot is exceptional — I was tasting samples with Diego this morning, the La Esperanza cherry notes are really pronounced.

How is everything with you? I heard there were some difficult months at Strauss with the market — hope your team is doing okay.

Alejandro

raw_emails.subject: Q1 shipment received — quality result
raw_emails.body:
Alejandro,

Shipment received. Our cupper scored the lot at 85.2 — really excellent. Your best shipment in three years, honestly. Production is very happy.

Invoice processing on our end.

David

raw_emails.subject: 85.2 — incredible! + Q2 discussion
raw_emails.body:
David!

This made our whole team very happy to hear. 85.2 — we will share this with the farmers at the next co-op meeting, they work so hard and it means a lot to get that feedback directly from the roaster. Diego said he knew when we were drying the La Esperanza lot that it was something special. He has a gift for reading the cherries.

My wife is reading over my shoulder as I write this and says to tell Sara hello. She still remembers the photos from your visit that you sent us.

On Q2 — the May-June harvest cycle is starting. The San Augustín micro-lot will be available for you — this is the one with the berry notes you liked when I sent the sample in October, do you remember? For the standard Q2 order we can do 10 tons again in May. Shall I ask Sofia to start the schedule?

Alejandro

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 discussion
raw_emails.body:
Alejandro,

Great to hear. Yes, 10 tons for Q2 works. Please ask Sofia to start the schedule.

On timing — we're hoping for arrival before June 15 due to a production scheduling deadline.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 timing
raw_emails.body:
David,

Sofia and I were just talking about this. The San Augustín harvest comes in around April 15, processing takes about 3 weeks after that. So earliest ship date from Bogotá would be around May 5-10 — Sofia will confirm. Sea transit is usually 20-22 days, so arrival at Ashdod would be late May at earliest.

June 15 should be achievable as long as there are no delays. Sofia will put together the schedule.

You know, I told my wife about your June 15 deadline and she said the same thing she always says — everything in procurement depends on something that is out of your hands! She used to work in logistics before she moved to teaching. She knows.

Alejandro

raw_emails.subject: Q2 shipment schedule confirmed
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

Confirming the Q2 schedule:
- Product ready: May 6
- Planned ship date from Bogotá: May 9
- ETA Ashdod: May 30-31

This is within your June 15 deadline. I will start the documentation this week.

Sofia

raw_emails.subject: The San Augustín lot + ship date update
raw_emails.body:
David,

I am writing from the farm today — the San Augustín plot is looking incredible, green and full. Diego and I were tasting the first dried samples this morning and the cherry notes are very pronounced, maybe even more than the La Esperanza from Q1. I think you will be very happy with this one.

Small update on the schedule — there was an equipment issue at the dry mill, a belt on the sorting machine that needed replacing. It pushed us back by 4 days. New ship date is May 13, so ETA at Ashdod becomes June 4-5. Still well within your June 15, no problem.

How is everything? I hope the spring is treating you and the family well.

Alejandro

raw_emails.subject: RE: Ship date
raw_emails.body:
Alejandro,

Things are fine here, thanks for asking. Good to hear the harvest is looking well.

June 4-5 arrival is still fine for our planning. Please send me the B/L when it is available.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 arrived — score is in
raw_emails.body:
Alejandro,

Shipment arrived June 6. Cup score: 85.8. Exceptional — best lot we have ever received from you. Sharing with the product team today.

Invoice being processed. Let's start thinking about Q3.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q3 — and some news from the farm
raw_emails.body:
David!

85.8 — I still cannot believe it. Diego printed the score sheet and put it on the wall of the drying station. The farmers here are so proud.

For Q3 — yes, absolutely. The August harvest from the La Esperanza plot is looking very promising again. We can plan 10 tons for August-September delivery. Sofia can start the documentation whenever you are ready.

One small thing — I want to make sure you know that the August shipment will also be invoiced in USD as always. If at that point the rate has moved in a way that creates a problem, please just tell me and we will figure something out together.

Send my best to your family.
Alejandro

raw_contracts.supplier_07_sierra_verde.md:
# Contract Summary — Sierra Verde Coffee Cooperative

**Contract ID:** STR-COFFEE-2024-003
**Supplier:** Sierra Verde Coffee Cooperative (Colombia)
**Category:** Coffee — green beans, Arabica, Huila region, 84+ cupping score (SCAA standard)
**Effective Date:** 2024-09-01
**Term:** 24 months
**Renewal Date:** 2026-08-31

## Payment Terms
Net 30 from export certificate date.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 8 tons/quarter, maximum 20 tons/quarter.

## Pricing
$4,200/ton at contract signing (September 2024), linked to ICO composite indicator price. Price adjustments require mutual written agreement with 30 days notice from either party. Current agreed price: **$4,450/ton** per written amendment dated 2025-03-15 (Amendment ref: STR-COFFEE-2024-003-A1).

## Penalty Clauses
- Late delivery beyond 10 calendar days past agreed delivery window: 0.5% of shipment value per week delayed (reflects long-standing cooperative relationship; more lenient than Strauss standard terms).
- Quality below 84 cupping score (SCAA): proportional price reduction calculated per SCAA grading scale applied to the non-conforming lot.

## Notes
Long-standing relationship supplier (3+ years). The email thread contains personal and relational content interspersed with business terms — the extraction logic should not skip over delivery dates, volumes, or pricing commitments embedded within informal or personal messages. No major structural dispute. However, delivery windows communicated in email are sometimes vague ("early next month," "around May 5-10") while this contract requires firm calendar dates; the prototype should flag any gap between email-stated delivery windows and the contract's delivery date requirements and prompt the manager to confirm exact dates ahead of each meeting.
